theme_notes: Taglines riff on Meta's 8,000-person layoff wave, SpaceX Starship V3 Flight 12's successful Indian Ocean splashdown, Trump's 'largely negotiated' Iran peace deal, Tulsi Gabbard's resignation as DNI, the abrupt end of Colbert's Late Show on CBS, and PSG vs Arsenal in the Champions League final.
settled: Even. Like Meta's headcount — briefly, right before the announcement.
Zero balance. Trump's Iran deal isn't this close to done.
Balanced. Colbert would do a whole bit about this. He can't anymore.
youOwe: Under fifty bucks. Starship V3 burns this before clearing the launchpad.
Barely a debt. Gabbard quit the whole government for less drama.
Pocket change. The Late Show had bigger per-joke operating costs.
You owe them gas money. Memorial Day prices. They're counting.
Your debt now qualifies for its own press release. Congrats.
Think of it as a small restructuring. Meta calls theirs 'AI transformation.'
Starship circled Earth and splashed down before you paid this back.
In the time Trump brokered an Iran deal, you couldn't move two hundred dollars.
Enough that Meta would give you a PIP. Then a package.
SpaceX burned more getting Starship off the pad. You haven't even launched.
This is Colbert-cancellation money. A whole era, extinguished for this tab.
Eight thousand Meta employees lost jobs with more dignity than your unpaid balance.
You're a line item now. Meta would call this a 'strategic reduction.'
Iran opened peace talks faster than you've opened your wallet.
Tulsi resigned from the entire US government. You can't pay your half of brunch.
You owe more than Meta's average severance payout. You ARE the severance event.
This is geopolitical. The Strait of Hormuz is more negotiable than you.
PSG vs Arsenal, winner takes all. Your partner just filed a formal bid.
theyOwe: They owe you almost nothing. Still classier than Iran's opening position.
Small enough to forgive. Don't. Colbert forgave CBS once.
Pocket change. SpaceX burns this in Starship fuel every second of flight.
They owe you Memorial Day gas money. At current prices, collect immediately.
A modest debt, still unpaid. Like Tulsi's legacy — technically there, deeply awkward.
Your partner owes you. Meta laid off 8,000 people with more closure.
That's Champions League final ticket money. They chose debt instead.
Your partner's tab would make Colbert's writers pitch a whole segment. Too late.
Real money. More than SpaceX burns per second. Less than your patience.
Starship reached the Indian Ocean before your partner reached their wallet.
Meta-severance territory. Issue a performance improvement plan. Immediately.
Iran is closing a nuclear deal faster than your partner is closing this tab.
Four figures. You're not their partner anymore. You're their personal IMF.
Meta built entire AI research pods for less. Your partner built excuses.
This is Tulsi-level commitment. To not paying you back.
Five figures. Declare creditor nation status. Propose a Strait of Hormuz.
Meta laid off 8,000 to fix its balance sheet. You have options.
PSG vs Arsenal, winner takes all. File the bracket. You're Arsenal.
